sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, a 54-year-old male, presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheek bone area of his face, which he describes as excruciating and similar to being stabbed with an ice pick.
The pain is triggered by certain temperatures, wind, and cold, and is not alleviated by ibuprofen, Motrin, or topical treatments such as tiger balm.
He has not noticed any sensory loss or weakness in the affected area, but does experience numbness on the right side of his face.
He has a history of occasional headaches, but no history of migraines or brain tumors.
He rates the pain as a 10 out of 10 in severity.
Past medical history: The patient has not mentioned any past medical conditions, treatments, or encounters.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: The patient takes Tylenol for occasional headaches.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient was able to follow the doctor's finger in both directions, showed symmetrical teeth, and could hear on both sides of his face.
The patient reported a little numbness on the right side of his face, and a dull sensation on the right side when the doctor poked him with a needle, but a sharp sensation on the left side.
Test Results: The patient's MRI appears to show a small blood vessel abutting and pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has symptoms consistent with trigeminal neuralgia, a condition where nerve compression causes shooting electrical pain in the face.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol 100mg twice daily for pain management.
The patient will undergo genetic testing to ensure the medication is safe for him.
He will return in one month to assess the effectiveness of the medication and discuss further treatment options if necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one month for a follow-up appointment.
